FITNESS · COMPARISON · MAY 2026

Fitagentic vs Pike13.

An honest comparison for small studios, personal training businesses, and swim/youth program operators. Pike13 has one of the friendliest CRM UXs in the category — and that simplicity is a real advantage. Fitagentic is the AI-native alternative for operators where lead response and retention matter more than scheduling simplicity.

Disclosure: Treetop Growth Strategy is the marketing and thought-leadership arm of Fitagentic.ai's parent company. We have a commercial interest in Fitagentic. Pike13 gets real wins below — that's how an honest comparison reads.
The TL;DR

The honest answer

Pike13 is the stronger choice if you value simple scheduling above all else, your business model is high-touch personal training where the trainer-client relationship is the product (and tech should stay out of the way), or you're a swim/youth/family program with complex enrollment patterns Pike13 handles well.

Fitagentic is the stronger choice if you're past 50 active clients and lead response or member retention is becoming a bottleneck — or if you want AI agents handling the admin and conversation work so trainers can train.

Where Pike13 wins

The Pike13 case

  • Friendliest UX in the category. Pike13 is one of the easiest gym CRMs to set up and run day-to-day. For owner-operators who want to spend zero time fighting their software, this is real.
  • Simple scheduling and billing. Recurring memberships, packages, drop-ins, simple class scheduling — clean and predictable.
  • Swim / youth / family program fit. Pike13 handles the enrollment patterns and family-account complexity of youth programs better than most general fitness CRMs.
  • Lower learning curve for non-technical owners. Less to misconfigure, less to break, less staff training.
  • Reasonable pricing for very small businesses. Entry tier is well-suited to single-trainer or 2-3 person operations.
Where Fitagentic wins

The Fitagentic case

  • AI-native architecture. Pike13 has marketing automation; Fitagentic has agents. Lead nurture is conversational; no-show recovery is automatic; retention is proactive.
  • Sub-60-second lead response. Pike13's lead capture forms route to email; the trainer responds when they have time. Fitagentic responds in seconds with a real conversation.
  • Trainer-time displacement. The single biggest opportunity for personal training studios above 50 clients — Fitagentic absorbs the lead, scheduling, and FAQ conversations so trainers can train.
  • Member retention agents. Pike13 has reporting; Fitagentic acts on it. Particularly valuable for personal training where one cancelled client is a meaningful revenue hit.
  • Scales beyond Pike13's sweet spot. Pike13 starts to feel limited around 200+ active clients; Fitagentic stays performant well into multi-location.
Side-by-side

Honest feature comparison

DimensionPike13Fitagentic
UX simplicity for non-technical ownerStrongerSimple but more powerful surface
Family + youth program enrollmentStrongerAdequate
Recurring billingSolidSolid
Lead response speed + qualityEmail-based, hoursStronger — under 60 seconds
No-show recoveryManualStronger — first-class workflow
Member retention agentsReporting onlyStronger
Trainer-time displacementModestStronger — material
Scales above 200 active clientsAdequateStronger
Software-line monthly cost$129+ scaling with clients$250-$1,000/loc
Total cost of ownership for growing studioLower until ~50 clientsStronger past ~50 clients
See Fitagentic running on a real personal training studio's data.
